_sources.discrepancies: Illinois has no voter ID requirement — voters affirm identity by signing the poll book. NCSL confirms 'no document required'. Voter registration deadline is 28 days before election by mail/online; same-day registration available during early voting and on Election Day.

earlyVoting.notes: Early voting begins 40 days before the election. Hours and locations vary by county. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting begins 40 days before the election. Hours and locations vary by county. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting begins 40 days before the general election. Permanent early voting sites and hours set by county clerks. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.

votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Illinois does not prohibit phones at polling places. Voters may not photograph their ballot or other voters. No electioneering within 100 feet of the polling place entrance.
additionalRules: No photo ID required — voters sign the poll book to verify identity.
Open primary: voters request whichever party's ballot they want without declaring affiliation.
Grace period registration available at the county clerk's office up to and including Election Day.
No runoffs in Illinois — the candidate with the most votes wins.

eligibility: Any registered voter may request a vote by mail ballot without providing a reason
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: 5 days before the election (received by county clerk)
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be postmarked by Election Day and received within 14 days after, or received by the county clerk by 7 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must be postmarked by Election Day and received within 14 days, or returned in person by poll closing on Election Day.
